c1.speaker: dot
c1.line: Gibbons. The town thinks the new lodger's our thief and the town is enjoying itself far too much about it. Sell me today. One sentence.

choices.line: If the lodger's our thief, I'll eat the front page — but tonight I find out what he was actually doing at that monument.
A stranger, a shovel, and a legend about treasure. It writes itself.

c3.speaker: dot
c3.line: Good. Stories that write themselves are usually writing fiction. Follow him, watch, and print what you SEE.

c3weak.speaker: dot
c3weak.line: It writes itself, does it? Then it doesn't need you, and I don't pay the desk. Go watch him with your own eyes tonight.

c4.speaker: dot
c4.line: Archie staked out a bakery for three nights once, over missing flour. Third night he fell asleep and the baker covered him with a coat. Solved it anyway — the coat smelled of the flour. Even his naps did journalism.
